asset_candidates.name: Machine-mode Control/Status Registers (CSRs)
asset_candidates.description: Privileged CPU registers (e.g., mstatus, mtvec, mepc, pmpcfg) that control execution privilege, interrupt handling, and memory protection.

asset_candidates.name: Debug unit control state
asset_candidates.description: Debug mode enable, halt request, and debug register state that governs whether the core can be externally debugged.

asset_candidates.name: Interrupt controller registers
asset_candidates.description: Interrupt enable, pending, and priority registers accessible via APB, controlling interrupt routing and core wake-up.

asset_candidates.name: ICache/Dcache control and performance counters
asset_candidates.description: Cache control registers and hit/miss counters accessible via cache control interfaces, potentially security-sensitive configuration.

subject_candidates.name: CPU data/instruction bus master
subject_candidates.description: Core-initiated load/store and instruction fetch requests that drive the system bus and access peripherals, CSRs, and memory.

subject_candidates.name: Debug/JTAG interface
subject_candidates.description: External debug master that can halt the core, read/write registers (GPRs, CSRs), memory, and inject instructions.

subject_candidates.name: Hardware Processing Engine (HWPE)
subject_candidates.description: Accelerator that can issue memory requests and potentially access shared peripherals or memory without CPU mediation.

subject_candidates.name: APB peripheral bus master
subject_candidates.description: APB initiator that reads/writes interrupt controller registers; could be the CPU or another bus master through a bridge.

subject_candidates.name: Test mode interface
subject_candidates.description: test_mode_i signal that may enable manufacturing/DFT access paths bypassing normal security controls.

operation_candidates.name: Read CSR
operation_candidates.description: Reading machine-mode control/status registers to observe their values.

operation_candidates.name: Write CSR
operation_candidates.description: Writing to CSRs to change privilege state, interrupt masks, or PMP entries.

operation_candidates.name: Debug read/write of core register/memory
operation_candidates.description: Using debug hardware to read or modify GPRs, CSRs, or memory while the core is halted or running.

operation_candidates.name: Configure interrupt controller
operation_candidates.description: Write to interrupt enable/pending/priority registers over APB to alter interrupt routing.

operation_candidates.name: Debug halt/resume control
operation_candidates.description: Asserting debug request to force the core into debug mode, bypassing normal execution flow.

operation_candidates.name: Cache control configuration
operation_candidates.description: Writing to cache control registers (enable, flush, bypass) to affect memory access behavior.

guard_candidates.name: Machine mode privilege check
guard_candidates.description: Hardware enforcement that certain CSR writes are only allowed in machine mode (mstatus.mpp, privilege level in mstatus).

guard_candidates.name: Debug authentication/authorization
guard_candidates.description: Signal or state that determines whether external debug requests are accepted (may be tied high or conditional).

guard_candidates.name: PMP (Physical Memory Protection) checks
guard_candidates.description: Address-range and permission checks for load/store instructions, enforcing access rules on memory regions.

guard_candidates.name: APB address decode & write protection
guard_candidates.description: APB slave-side decode that only allows writes to specific register addresses; may gate writes with a lock or enable signal.

guard_candidates.name: Test mode lock
guard_candidates.description: A register bit or static tie-off that disables test-mode access after manufacturing, preventing runtime abuse of DFT paths.

guard_candidates.name: Cache control enable/clear gating
guard_candidates.description: Signals like ctrl_enable_regs, ctrl_clear_regs that gate updates to performance counters and cache state.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: Debug mode entry state
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Debug mode is entered when debug_req is asserted while core is in a particular state; remains until dret is executed. Reset value: debug mode off.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: mstatus.mie (machine interrupt enable) lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Set/cleared by CSR write; affected by xRET instructions; reset to 0 at power-up.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: PMP configuration lock bit
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Once set, PMP entries become read-only until next reset; reset value: unlocked (0).

state_lifecycle_facts.name: Interrupt enable register reset value
state_lifecycle_facts.description: Interrupt enable bits default to 0 after reset, blocking all interrupts until configured.

state_lifecycle_facts.name: Test mode signal default
state_lifecycle_facts.description: test_mode_i typically tied low during functional operation; if asserted, may bypass normal protections.

path_candidates.name: Debug unit -> CSR read/write
path_candidates.description: Debug hardware can access CSRs (e.g., DMI) without CPU privilege checks if not properly gated.

path_candidates.name: CPU (User mode) -> CSR write
path_candidates.description: A user-mode program may attempt to write protected CSRs; privilege check must block the write or cause an illegal instruction exception.

path_candidates.name: APB master -> interrupt controller register write
path_candidates.description: Any APB master with correct address decode can write interrupt enable/pending registers; missing lock/mediation could allow reconfiguration.

path_candidates.name: Test mode entry -> bypass normal register access controls
path_candidates.description: Asserting test_mode_i might allow unrestricted read/write of internal registers, ignoring APB access policy.

path_candidates.name: HWPE -> shared memory/peripheral access
path_candidates.description: Hardware accelerator may access memory or peripherals outside of CPU-visible address map, possibly bypassing PMP or security attributes.

summary: Identified critical assets include privileged CSRs, debug unit state, interrupt controller registers, and cache controls. Key subjects are CPU cores, debug/JTAG, HWPE accelerators, and test interfaces. Operations focus on read/write of sensitive registers, debug access, and configuration changes. Guards include privilege enforcement, debug authentication, PMP, APB write protection, and test-mode lock. State lifecycles cover debug entry conditions, CSR lock bits, and reset defaults. Potential paths of concern involve debug access to CSRs, user-mode CSR writes, uncontrolled APB access, test-mode bypass, and HWPE resource access without full mediation.
